textDisplay:
@DogDayFromPoppyPlaytime180 Ill give you here what you already read..and could only say..it was better than scream...who cares scream was awful too.

I'll take totally killer and megan. As the best slasher movies of the year so far. Way better than this.
At least they were more concerned with character development and good acting plus the gore. 
This movie had none of that. Those plots were much better too. At least a little more original. Megan of course has its premise sort of lifted from chucky. In that's its a doll. But killer dolls go way before chucky too. And when they combined the comedy they got it right.
Plus that doll was a little scary, the killer in thanksgiving was laughable. I would take him out in 10 seconds. That doll I wouldn't wanna F$@$ with. 
There was subtext and complexity 
Thanksgiving was just gore for gores sake. Nothing original. Derivative of every slasher movie with nothing new to offer.
I didnt care about these characters at all. 
Very poorly written. 
We saw meaningless characters killed in 80s Slashers at least try to make us care for them now. Halloween was the one that did back then. 
So just getting a bunch of poorly written and under developed characters with no depth together, who I don't even care about for a slaughter is just boring for me now. Oh wow did you see that kill, what the killer did and wow the body count..lol Yes the return of the slasher lol. If you like not even caring about who he kills. 

If that's what youre interested in than you just going for gore. 
Another thing, I do enjoy a good revenge movie. This wasnt one of them. 
The wrath of becky was better in that genre this year.

textDisplay: Saw it yesterday and it’s one of the best, most original horror/slasher movies I’ve seen in a long time. It had me completely invested from start to finish with no dull moments really. It’s a really intense movie that you should unquestionably see in the theatre. I didn’t watch any trailers or read any reviews before watching the movie, so I didn’t really know what to expect. It’s not often that I give a movie full stars but this one gets a  5/5 ⭐️ rating from me.

DO NOT READ ANY COMMENT BELOW THIS! In Most Recent Comments - 

I came here to see is it worth a worth in the Cinema today, It's Sunday I'm not doing much said I'd see the Recent comment's As it's only just out
And after 2 or 3 comments ,Its a bunch of kid's or Fucking Idiots Saying X Y & Z Laughing about it ( Not saying what they said )

I seen 3 comments saying it's Really good worth Watch for sure! ,  2 said not great  , And One other person said This .... 

" It's a Mix between I Know what you did last summer meet Final Destination With a Flare of Jeepers Creepers " And this is Why I'm going to see it

In hindsight i should have stopped right there , As that was all i needed to know. So if you want Spoilers go below my comment, But I'm going to give it a Try as I Love Late 90's early 2000's Horror ( Nostalgia reasons very early teen days for me ) 

So I'm going off its IMDB score of 7.1 out of ( 2,623 )Votes of People who seen the Movie & Voted, That's a Really Good score,

And From Rotten Tomatoes it's 83% Fresh ( out 86 Critic Reviews ) & 79% Out Of all Audience Score ( Which is Good imo )

It starts at the Cinema here in 2 Hours So I'm Giving this Movie a Go & Ill give a Tiny edit & score at the bottom when i get home. I do Love a Good Slasher & Horror And no Harm in a bit of comedy at times, So iv very open Going in,, Ill Come back & Give a score out of 10

textDisplay:
A table descended from heaven and it consisted of 7 huge loaves of bread, 7 huge cooked whales, not turkey.
[5:109]
In the day when Allah gathereth together the messengers, and saith: What was your response (from mankind) ? they say: We have no knowledge. Lo! Thou, only Thou art the Knower of Things Hidden,

[5:110]
When Allah saith: O Jesus, son of Mary! Remember My favour unto thee and unto thy mother; how I strengthened thee with the holy Spirit, so that thou spakest unto mankind in the cradle as in maturity; and how I taught thee the Scripture and Wisdom and the Torah and the Gospel; and how thou didst shape of clay as it were the likeness of a bird by My permission, and didst blow upon it and it was a bird by My permission, and thou didst heal him who was born blind and the leper by My permission; and how thou didst raise the dead by My permission; and how I restrained the Children of Israel from (harming) thee when thou camest unto them with clear proofs, and those of them who disbelieved exclaimed: This is naught else than mere magic;

[5:111]
And when I inspired the disciples, (saying): Believe in Me and in My messenger, they said: We believe. Bear witness that we have surrendered (unto Thee) "we are muslims".

[5:112]
When the disciples said: O Jesus, son of Mary! Is thy Lord able to send down for us a table spread with food from heaven ? He said: Observe your duty to Allah, if ye are true believers.

[5:113]
(They said:) We wish to eat thereof, that we may satisfy our hearts and know that thou hast spoken truth to us, and that thereof we may be witnesses.

[5:114]
Jesus, son of Mary, said: O Allah, Lord of us! Send down for us a table spread with food from heaven, that it may be a feast for us, for the first of us and for the last of us, and a sign from Thee. Give us sustenance, for Thou art the Best of Sustainers.

[5:115]
Allah said: Lo! I send it down for you. And whoso disbelieveth of you afterward, him surely will I punish with a punishment wherewith I have not punished any of (My) creatures.

[5:116]
And when Allah saith: O Jesus, son of Mary! Didst thou say unto mankind: Take me and my mother for two gods beside Allah ? he saith: Be glorified! It was not mine to utter that to which I had no right. If I used to say it, then Thou knewest it. Thou knowest what is in my mind, and I know not what is in Thy Mind. Lo! Thou, only Thou, art the Knower of Things Hidden ?

[5:117]
I spake unto them only that which Thou commandedst me, (saying): Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord. I was a witness of them while I dwelt among them, and when Thou tookest me Thou wast the Watcher over them. Thou art Witness over all things.

[5:118]
If Thou punish them, lo! they are Thy slaves, and if Thou forgive them (lo! they are Thy slaves). Lo! Thou, only Thou, art the Mighty, the Wise.

[5:119]
Allah saith: This is a day in which their truthfulness profiteth the truthful, for theirs are Gardens underneath which rivers flow, wherein they are secure for ever, Allah taking pleasure in them and they in Him. That is the great triumph.

[5:120]
Unto Allah belongeth the Sovereignty of the heavens and the earth and whatsoever is therein, and He is Able to do all things.

textDisplay:
@jackwest2551 Rotten tomatoes to its credit gets it right most times. Most times. Not always imo. 
82 doesnt make it awesome. It makes it good. Anything in the 90s is in the awesome range.
Then if you look at the top critics which is where I really go, and not just the reviewers from any old never heard of source, that doesnt have much credibility, that's also something to consider. There is sometimes a difference of opinion. 
You also have audience's ratings.
Which can differ from critics and vice versa. 
I tend to go with reviewers over audiences. 

Have I found movies that were rated highly and I thought were bad, yes. 
Have I found some lower rated movies I thought were better, yes. 
This one falls in the 1st category.

textDisplay:
Wow, just got out of the theatre seeing this. I imagine this is how some might have felt in 96' watching Scream opening night or in 78' with Halloween. THANKSGIVING is so aggressively over-the-top, but at least it knows & embraces it. Eli Roth’s slasher film features some of the most gruesome & hilarious kills of the year. Silly & absurd B-movie horror that can be a killer good time if you’re open to it. He really blessed us with one of the best slashers in modern memory, hilariously over the top blood soaked carnage!

To think that the premise for the thanksgiving theme around this goofy holiday slasher was originally just teased as a fake trailer teaser from the “Grindhouse” double feature by Quentin Tarantino & Robert Rodriguez’s infamous 2007 feat is quite impressive. 🦃🩸🍴🔪🍽️

Thanksgiving feels major. It's definitely the slasher of the year, but I mean looking back, it's the best in years. A new classic ripped straight out of the golden age of slashers. I felt like I was watching the culmination of someone's life work. Roth has said many times that he's been wanting to make this movie since he was 12, and I could feel every bit of that passionate energy jump off the screen. It felt like Roth was emptying out all of his pockets and throwing out all of the tricks that he had been saving for years. Every other moment felt like it was attempting to one-up the last. This flick is filled to the brim. I have no idea how the rest of the world will receive this film, but this completely blew me away. Thanksgiving was everything I wanted and more, Roth completely delivered on all fronts. I can only predict it will be trashed and forgotten like the rest of Eli Roth's work, but perhaps just maybe others will agree with me.

If you’re a horror fan and love slashers give this one a try, the trailers didn’t do its full justice. 🔥

textDisplay: Let me ask this question: Does anyone else remember Grindhouse, where there was a double feature: Planet Terror and Deathproof 3000? Well, if not, nevermind about those two movies, but in between those movies were a series of trailer, a lot like the ones in actual movie theaters. One of those trailers was for a movie called Thanksgiving, with the same theme as this. The end of the trailer has stayed with me since then; it's that mind-blowing.
textOriginal: Let me ask this question: Does anyone else remember Grindhouse, where there was a double feature: Planet Terror and Deathproof 3000? Well, if not, nevermind about those two movies, but in between those movies were a series of trailer, a lot like the ones in actual movie theaters. One of those trailers was for a movie called Thanksgiving, with the same theme as this. The end of the trailer has stayed with me since then; it's that mind-blowing.

textDisplay: Uh, I will give this a chance. Before I came and watched the trailer, I expected the movie to be closer to the fake trailer first shown in between the "Grindhouse Tarantino/Rodriguez" movies, I was expecting it to be shot on 16 mm or at least made to look like it was, I was expecting it to take place in the 1970's and I was expecting it to look grimy, none that is in this trailer, it is shot on flat digital and has none of the charm and humor of the original fake trailer. I am not a Roth fan to begin with but this might prove me wrong, right now I am kind of worried. I'll wait for the reviews from the audience.
textOriginal: Uh, I will give this a chance. Before I came and watched the trailer, I expected the movie to be closer to the fake trailer first shown in between the "Grindhouse Tarantino/Rodriguez" movies, I was expecting it to be shot on 16 mm or at least made to look like it was, I was expecting it to take place in the 1970's and I was expecting it to look grimy, none that is in this trailer, it is shot on flat digital and has none of the charm and humor of the original fake trailer. textDisplay:
I hate to say it, but this fully realized film feels like it lost something that the original fake trailer in Grindhouse had in abundance. Judging from the comments most people dont even remember that. It has been a while, and the Grindhouse double feature kinda bombed so most people didnt see those trailers.
For me, the authentically retro vibe, look and feel the og trailer really added to it, and now that it's a full film it seems to be gone. Sad for me, but still looks great, dont get me wrong. Machete retained that vibe for the most part once it became a fully realized film, too bad this doesnt seem to have. Oh well.
textOriginal:
I hate to say it, but this fully realized film feels like it lost something that the original fake trailer in Grindhouse had in abundance. Judging from the comments most people dont even remember that. It has been a while, and the Grindhouse double feature kinda bombed so most people didnt see those trailers.
For me, the authentically retro vibe, look and feel the og trailer really added to it, and now that it's a full film it seems to be gone. Sad for me, but still looks great, dont get me wrong. Machete retained that vibe for the most part once it became a fully realized film, too bad this doesnt seem to have. Oh well.

textDisplay: So many derided the franchise rules Mindy said in the newest Scream movie but I saw a few already speak of how to create a whole franchise out of this movie and it's not out yet. The worst thing that happened to so many slasher movies is them spawning villain-based franchises with the worst of them being Friday the 13th due to how stale it grew and how quickly it did so. It's why a requel will never work. Scream isn't fully a villain-based franchise and it'll push through with a 7th successful movie staying that way. This movie is better off not being a villain-based franchise if any at all. With that, I'll see it in the theaters.
textOriginal: So many derided the franchise rules Mindy said in the newest Scream movie but I saw a few already speak of how to create a whole franchise out of this movie and it's not out yet. The worst thing that happened to so many slasher movies is them spawning villain-based franchises with the worst of them being Friday the 13th due to how stale it grew and how quickly it did so. It's why a requel will never work. Scream isn't fully a villain-based franchise and it'll push through with a 7th successful movie staying that way. This movie is better off not being a villain-based franchise if any at all. With that, I'll see it in the theaters.

textDisplay:
I’m so glad that Grindhouse still has an influence in Hollywood! If people don’t know what I’m talking about, this film is based on the fake trailer that was a part of Grindhouse, a double feature that presented Planet Terror and Death Proof! If people are confused when I say fake trailer, a series of fake trailers were made specifically for Grindhouse! Machete, Hobo With A Shotgun, Don’t, Werewolf Women Of The SS, and Thanksgiving!

As of right now, Machete, Hobo With A Shotgun, and the upcoming Thanksgiving Film have all received their own films! Cannot wait for any future project based on Grindhouse!
textOriginal:
I’m so glad that Grindhouse still has an influence in Hollywood! If people don’t know what I’m talking about, this film is based on the fake trailer that was a part of Grindhouse, a double feature that presented Planet Terror and Death Proof! If people are confused when I say fake trailer, a series of fake trailers were made specifically for Grindhouse! Machete, Hobo With A Shotgun, Don’t, Werewolf Women Of The SS, and Thanksgiving!

As of right now, Machete, Hobo With A Shotgun, and the upcoming Thanksgiving Film have all received their own films! Cannot wait for any future project based on Grindhouse!

textDisplay: Im not painting a group of people as violent nor am I insinuating any connection to reality, but u gotta admit, the most obvious plot behind the mask would b a pissed off Native American Indian. It cant happen in a p.c. world, but considering the importance of providing empathy for the protagonist in any violent story, this narrative would make the most sense across the board. Maybe a white bread town that grew over the top of an Indian village where families were displaced & then generational oppression building up angst amongst the living ancestors???

textDisplay:
FINALLY!! 

There’s a Thanksgiving Killer! You know how many awesome ideas they could have came up with??? In like the fucking decades??? 

NO! Christmas, Halloween, New Years Day, Valentines Day, St Patty’s Day, 4th of July, Birthday! Summer, Winter, Spring, & Fall! 

All the fucking seasons, and the holidays have a killer EXCEPT for Thanksgiving, what.. hehe. What gives? 😂🥁

NOW IN 20-fucking-23! We’re JUST NOW THINKING????

“Geee, you know what sounds fun? Chomping up some people in Thanksgiving? Sounds like a blast!” 😃 

But late then never!
textOriginal:
FINALLY!! 

There’s a Thanksgiving Killer! You know how many awesome ideas they could have came up with??? In like the fucking decades??? 

NO! Christmas, Halloween, New Years Day, Valentines Day, St Patty’s Day, 4th of July, Birthday! Summer, Winter, Spring, & Fall! 

All the fucking seasons, and the holidays have a killer EXCEPT for Thanksgiving, what.. hehe. What gives? 😂🥁

NOW IN 20-fucking-23! We’re JUST NOW THINKING????

“Geee, you know what sounds fun? Chomping up some people in Thanksgiving? Sounds like a blast!” 😃 

But late then never!

textDisplay:
If a serial killer was after me, I’d be on the first flight out of the country without telling anyone. It would be almost impossible for the killer to stay anonymous if they have to use their passport to follow after me (if they could even figure out where I was). After all small town means a small population. Cut that down further by who left the country & the suspect list suddenly becomes quite short. 

There’s just no frickin way I’d stay after knowing I’d been tagged & people had already been killed. They aren’t the FBI or CIA, if I leave town & cut all contact it becomes extremely difficult to track me down.
